_meta.purpose: Level-up + TM-compat learnsets for the team-relevant FireRed species. The TeamPlanner reads this for two decisions: (1) evolve/level TIMING to pick up a key level-up move (e.g. Venusaur's Razor Leaf @L20, Sleep Powder) - B-to-cancel a bad learn, or grind to the level that grants the move she wants; (2) can a planned keeper LEARN a coverage TM (the tm[] list) - so 'teach Lapras Ice Beam / Thunderbolt', 'teach Snorlax Earthquake', 'teach Alakazam Psychic/Shadow Ball' are validated before the plan commits a single-use TM.
_meta.source: Bulbapedia species learnset pages (Gen-3 FRLG) + long-standing knowledge, distilled offline. level_up levels are best-effort (verify the exact level organically when she learns it live); the tm[] compat lists are the load-bearing planner data and are authored carefully.
_meta.schema: species: {level_up: [[level, move], ...] (the notable/keeper moves, not every filler), tm: [tm_ids the mon can learn among the COVERAGE set], note?: str}. tm ids match frlg_tms.json.
_meta.coverage_tms_tracked: TM13 ice-beam, TM24 thunderbolt, TM25 thunder, TM26 earthquake, TM28 dig, TM29 psychic, TM30 shadow-ball, TM35 flamethrower, TM38 fire-blast, TM22 solar-beam, TM31 brick-break, TM40 aerial-ace, TM19 giga-drain, TM06 toxic, TM03 water-pulse, TM34 shock-wave, TM23 iron-tail, HM03 surf, HM04 strength, HM01 cut.

bulbasaur.note: starter; Razor Leaf @20 is her first real STAB - plan grinds to it

venusaur.note: evolves from ivysaur @32; SolarBeam is the big grass nuke (pair with Sunny Day); learns Cut for gate use. Grass STAB resisted by Poison/Steel - needs coverage teammate vs Koga

abra.note: ONLY knows Teleport until it evolves - catch it and grind to L16, cannot attack meanwhile (lead with a partner)

kadabra.note: evolves from abra @16; Alakazam needs a TRADE (unavailable solo) so Kadabra stays as-is - STILL an elite special sweeper. Psybeam is fine until TM29 Psychic

alakazam.note: trade-evo (solo run keeps Kadabra); THE coverage platform - Psychic + Ice Beam + Thunderbolt + Shadow Ball on one glass cannon

diglett.note: learns Dig @17 (STAB before the TM); Earthquake @41 by level OR TM26

dugtrio.note: evolves from diglett @26; the clean Surge answer + an Earthquake user for Koga/Blaine/Agatha

growlithe.note: evolve to Arcanine with a Fire Stone - EVOLVE LATE (Arcanine's level-up pool is thin; keep Growlithe till it has the moves you want, or just TM Flamethrower)

arcanine.note: Fire-Stone evo; learns almost all its offense via TM - teach Flamethrower (TM35). The Erika sweep + fire coverage

lapras.note: Silph gift; the E4 MVP - Ice Beam (TM13 or L43) is the Lance dragon-slayer, Thunderbolt (TM24) hits Gyarados/Lorelei, Surf is STAB. Water/Ice tank

snorlax.note: static (Poke Flute); a colossal Normal wall that TMs almost anything - Earthquake (TM26) + Ice Beam (TM13) + Body Slam make it a coverage tank

pikachu.note: evolve to Raichu with a Thunder Stone AFTER it learns Thunderbolt @26 (Raichu's level pool is empty - keep Pikachu till 26, then stone)

raichu.note: Thunder-Stone evo; TM-driven - Thunderbolt/Thunder. An electric answer for Lorelei/Misty/Gyarados

gastly.note: Ghost/Poison; -> Haunter @25 -> Gengar (TRADE, unavailable solo). Haunter is still a strong Sabrina/Agatha answer with Shadow Ball (TM30)

haunter.note: trade-evo to Gengar (solo keeps Haunter); Shadow Ball gives it the Bug/Ghost STAB Sabrina fears

magikarp.note: useless until Gyarados @20 - a project, not a coverage pick

gyarados.note: Water/Flying powerhouse from Magikarp @20; Thunderbolt covers its own 4x weakness discussion aside, a strong physical/water body

pidgey.note: early Flying; -> Pidgeotto @18 -> Pidgeot @36. Fly (HM02) utility + Aerial Ace (TM40) vs Erika/bugs

pidgeot.note: evolves from pidgeotto @36; Fly slot + a flying answer for Erika/Bruno

eevee.note: gift; stone-evolve into the coverage hole - Vaporeon (water), Jolteon (electric), Flareon (fire). Jolteon = a fast electric for Lorelei/Lance's Gyarados
